data.title: Pi Coin Trades Within Range Amid Weak Reversal Signals
data.description: Pi Coin is currently trading at $0.170, maintaining a range between a resistance level of $0.209 and a support level of $0.162 since mid-March. According to NS3.AI, a bullish RSI divergence has emerged on the 12-hour chart. However, the potential for a sustained reversal is undermined by decreasing trading volume and a 0.35 year-on-year Pearson correlation with Bitcoin.

data.title: DoorDash to Integrate Tempo Blockchain for Global Payments
data.description: DoorDash is set to implement the Tempo blockchain to facilitate payments to its Dashers, expanding its reach across more than 40 countries. According to NS3.AI, Tempo's public mainnet was launched in March 2026 following a $500 million funding round that valued the company at $5 billion. While DoorDash has announced its plans, a specific launch date for the integration has not yet been established.

data.title: Turkey's Central Bank Maintains Key Repo Rate at 37.00%
data.description: Turkey's central bank has decided to keep its benchmark repo rate unchanged at 37.00%, aligning with market expectations. According to Jin10, this decision reflects the bank's ongoing strategy to manage inflation and stabilize the economy. The central bank's move comes amid a challenging economic environment, with inflationary pressures and global economic uncertainties influencing monetary policy decisions. By maintaining the current rate, the central bank aims to support economic stability while addressing inflation concerns.

data.title: Russia Considers Additional Tax on Commodity Producers and Banks
data.description: Russia is contemplating imposing an extra tax on certain commodity producers and banks to address budget deficits arising from increased war-related expenditures, according to informed sources. The Kremlin has not yet made a final decision, and detailed discussions are expected to occur later this year with the commencement of a new budget cycle. Potential targets for the tax include major commodity companies such as top gold producer Polyus PJSC and mining giant Norilsk Nickel, as well as some private banks. According to Jin10, Russia's economy experienced a slowdown last year due to expanding budget deficits, prompting the government to seek additional revenue sources, including raising the value-added tax rate starting this year.

data.title: Coinbase moves New York prediction market lawsuit to federal court
data.description: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal stated that the company has moved the lawsuit filed by New York Attorney General Letitia James against its prediction market business from state court to federal court, citing federal legal disputes. The lawsuit alleges that Coinbase and Gemini's prediction market products violated New York State gambling laws by allowing users to trade sports, entertainment, and election events without a license. Coinbase maintains that prediction markets should be uniformly regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and will continue to push for federal regulatory approval. The case involves the division of state and federal regulatory authority and could significantly impact the regulatory framework for prediction market platforms. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: U.S. Defense Budget for 2027 Sees Significant Increase
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Defense's acting auditor general, Jules Hurst, announced on April 21 the defense budget for the 2027 fiscal year under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration, totaling approximately $1.5 trillion. According to Jin10, this represents a 42% increase from the previous year. The budget focuses on purchasing and investing in hardware, with about 52% allocated for ammunition, aircraft, tanks, and ships. It also includes investments in the 'Golden Dome' missile defense system, drones, and space superiority to ensure national security and military dominance.

U.S. sources indicate that investment in drones and related technologies will triple to over $74 billion. Additionally, more than $30 billion is expected to be spent on procuring critical munitions, including missile interceptors. New military projects promoted by the Trump administration, such as funding for the F-47 fighter jet and the envisioned 'Golden Fleet,' will also receive substantial financial support, with hundreds of billions of dollars allocated. The 'Golden Dome' missile defense system is projected to receive $18 billion in funding.

If approved by the U.S. Congress, this budget proposal would mark the largest annual increase in U.S. military spending since the end of World War II, according to U.S. sources.

data.title: MacSync Stealer Malware Targets macOS Users
data.description:
A malicious software named 'MacSync Stealer' (v1.1.2) is currently active and highly destructive, according to BlockBeats. This malware targets macOS users, stealing sensitive data such as cryptocurrency wallets, browser credentials, system keychains, and infrastructure keys like SSH, AWS, and K8s.

The malware employs fake AppleScript system dialogs for phishing and displays a false 'unsupported' error message after data is leaked. The indicators of compromise (IOC) have been promptly shared with clients.

Users are advised to avoid executing unverified macOS scripts and remain vigilant against unexpected system password prompts. In case of a suspected attack, immediate remediation is necessary: change all infrastructure credentials (SSH, AWS, K8s), invalidate exposed keychains, and swiftly transfer cryptocurrency assets to secure wallets.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Likely to Maintain Rates, Deutsche Bank Strategists Say
data.description: On April 22, Jin10 reported that numerous media outlets suggest the Bank of Japan may keep interest rates unchanged next week. This has led Deutsche Bank strategists, including Alex Lu, to postpone their rate hike expectations to June. According to Jin10, Lu and his colleagues believe the Bank of Japan is signaling through media that it has decided to delay rate hikes to avoid surprising the market. Market pricing indicates a high threshold for unexpected changes, but despite the possibility of the Bank of Japan waiting to assess the impact of the Middle East conflict, maintaining rates is seen as a pause in the rate hike cycle rather than its conclusion. Economic conditions necessitate further policy tightening, as monetary policy remains very loose. The pricing of terminal rates remains above 1.5%, reflecting the market's view that keeping rates unchanged merely postpones the issue.

data.title: AT&T Exceeds Market Expectations with Wireless Postpaid Phone User Growth
data.description: AT&T reported a net addition of 294,000 wireless postpaid phone users in the first quarter, surpassing market expectations of 262,200. According to Jin10, this growth highlights AT&T's strong performance in the competitive telecommunications sector.

data.title: Germany's Economy Shows Modest Growth Amid Challenges
data.description: Germany's economy likely experienced slight growth in the first quarter, despite facing challenges from the Iran war and persistent structural issues, according to the Bundesbank. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic hurdles that continue to impact the country's financial landscape. The Bundesbank's assessment suggests that while there is some positive movement, significant obstacles remain that could affect future economic performance. The ongoing conflict and internal economic challenges are key factors influencing Germany's economic trajectory.

data.title: Boston Scientific Lowers Full-Year Earnings Forecast
data.description: Boston Scientific has revised its full-year adjusted earnings per share forecast to a range of $3.34 to $3.41, which falls short of market expectations of $3.45. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects the company's updated financial outlook for the year.

data.title: STOCKS | Boston Scientific Shares Drop 3.9% Pre-Market Following Full-Year Outlook Revision
data.description: Boston Scientific's stock experienced a 3.9% decline in pre-market trading after the company revised its full-year performance expectations. According to Jin10, the medical device manufacturer adjusted its forecast, impacting investor sentiment and leading to the drop in share price. The revision reflects changes in market conditions and company strategy, prompting investors to reassess their positions. Boston Scientific's adjustment highlights the challenges faced by companies in maintaining growth projections amidst evolving industry dynamics.

data.title: Bitcoin's Bull Score Index Reaches Neutral Territory as BTC Approaches $78,000
data.description: Bitcoin's Bull Score Index has risen to 50, indicating a move into neutral territory as the cryptocurrency's price approaches $78,000. According to NS3.AI, this marks the first time since October last year that the index has reached this level. CryptoQuant contributor Julio Moreno noted that a similar shift into neutral territory occurred in March 2022, which lasted about a week before Bitcoin continued its downward trend.

data.title: AI TRENDS | NeoCognition Secures $40 Million in Seed Funding to Enhance AI Reliability
data.description:
NeoCognition, an AI research laboratory, has announced the completion of a $40 million seed funding round, led by Cambium Capital and Walden Catalyst Ventures, with participation from Vista Equity Partners. According to Odaily, angel investors include Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica.

Based in Palo Alto, California, NeoCognition was founded by Ohio State University Associate Professor Yu Su, along with Xiang Deng and Yu Gu. The company focuses on improving the reliability of AI agents by enabling them to build domain-specific 'world models' for practical use, rather than relying solely on general pre-trained models. Currently targeting enterprise clients, NeoCognition plans to integrate sustainably optimized AI agents into SaaS products, although no public product has been released yet.

data.title: GMGN Co-founder: The web version now supports ETH limit orders and multi-wallet trading.
data.description: GMGN co-founder Haze posted on the X platform that the web version has completed several feature updates, including support for ETH limit orders and advanced strategies, support for multi-wallet profit and loss (PNL) statistics, support for multi-wallet trading and continuous click trading, and has completed the first round of speed-up optimizations, and will continue to be improved.

data.title: AI research lab NeoCognition has raised $40 million in seed funding, led by Cambium Capital and others.
data.description: AI research lab NeoCognition announced the completion of a $40 million seed funding round, co-led by Cambium Capital and Walden Catalyst Ventures, with participation from Vista Equity Partners. Angel investors include Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica. Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, the company was co-founded by Ohio State University Associate Professor Yu Su, along with Xiang Deng and Yu Gu, and focuses on improving the reliability of AI agents. Their solution involves allowing agents to build domain-specific "world models" in real-world use to adapt to specific business environments, rather than relying on general pre-trained models. Currently targeting enterprise clients, the company plans to embed sustainably optimized AI agents into its SaaS products; no publicly released product has yet been launched.

data.title: Prediction Market Shows Bets Against Atlanta United Victory in April 2026
data.description:
A monitoring report indicates that an account with profits exceeding $220,000 has invested approximately $42,700 in a position against Atlanta United's victory over New England Revolution on April 22, 2026. According to Odaily, the opening price for this position was 54.9¢, and the current price stands at 55.5¢, resulting in a floating profit of about $477.

Atlanta United has been struggling recently, securing only one win in their last eight matches and suffering three consecutive defeats. In contrast, New England Revolution has achieved four consecutive victories, demonstrating solid defensive performance. The historical match record between the two teams is closely matched, and injuries are influencing market expectations, which remain relatively balanced.

data.title: ECB's Simkus: Rate Hike Possible This Year
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Simkus has stated that the possibility of an interest rate hike by the ECB this year cannot be ruled out. According to Jin10, Simkus emphasized the need to remain vigilant regarding inflationary pressures and economic developments. The ECB has been closely monitoring the economic situation in the Eurozone, and any decision on interest rates will depend on future data and economic conditions. Simkus's comments highlight the ongoing uncertainty in monetary policy as the ECB navigates the challenges posed by inflation and economic growth.

data.title: Greece Introduces €500 Million Economic Relief Following Budget Success
data.description: Greece has unveiled new economic relief measures totaling approximately €500 million. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows the country's budget performance, which exceeded expectations last year. The Greek government aims to support its citizens and stimulate economic growth with these measures. The relief package is part of a broader strategy to maintain fiscal stability while addressing economic challenges. The announcement underscores Greece's commitment to sustaining its economic recovery and providing assistance to those in need.

data.title: Another cargo ship was attacked while attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: On April 22 local time, Vanguard reported that a cargo ship was attacked while attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz, marking the third vessel attacked that day. The Panamanian-flagged cargo ship, the "MSC Francesca," was reportedly attacked approximately 6 nautical miles off the coast of Iran while en route south through the Strait of Hormuz into the Gulf of Oman. The vessel reported damage to its hull and living quarters. Earlier that day, the UK's Office for Maritime Trade Operations had released reports of attacks on two other vessels in the same area. (CCTV News) (Jinshi Data APP)
